title: Polymer Melt Flow Index Automated Tester
description: ASTM D1238-compliant MFI tester that heats a polymer barrel to setpoint via PID, monitors piston displacement with LVDT to compute MFI automatically, measures extrudate colour for degradation detection, and logs all data to serial. Differentiates from similar controllers by combining real-time rheological measurement with optical degradation sensing.
objective: Heat barrel to 190 °C (PE) ±1 °C; automatically compute MFI from piston displacement over 10 min; detect degradation via yellowness index; fault-cut heater on overtemperature or degradation.
constraints: Arduino Mega; ceramic band heater 230 V/300 W; SSR; LVDT; TCS34725 colour sensor; BOM < $85.
supporting_fact_titles: Polymer Melt Flow Index and Viscosity Temperature Dependence
design_element_titles: RGB Colour Sensor TCS34725 Degradation Detector
element_usage_notes: Band heater PID-controlled via SSR; Type-J TC on barrel OD as process variable
HX711 verifies 2.16 kg standard mass is correctly applied before test start
LVDT measures piston displacement every 1 s; displacement/time → MFI
TCS34725 samples extrudate colour every 30 s; high yellowness index triggers degradation fault

element_interactions.description: PID temperature settled at setpoint for 5 min before LVDT displacement measurement begins

element_interactions.to: RGB Colour Sensor TCS34725 Degradation Detector
element_interactions.description: LVDT confirms melt flowing; colour sensor samples extrudate strand for yellowing
Load cell verifies standard mass applied before heater is enabled for test

element_interactions.from: RGB Colour Sensor TCS34725 Degradation Detector
element_interactions.description: High yellowness index triggers heater cutoff via fault handler
